title: boechera stricta
intro: Boechera stricta, also known as Alpine Rockcress, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and a distinctive, pungent smell. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of isothiocyanates and other compounds. Boechera stricta has been used to support the health of the respiratory system, as well as to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. It can be prepared as a tea or tincture, or used fresh in salads, and has also been used in traditional herbal medicine to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
